audit_items.proof_trace: All allocations in parse_definition are held by RAII smart pointers (Parameters_Obj = SharedImpl<Parameters>, Block_Obj = SharedImpl<Block>), which are stack-local variables whose destructors call decRefCount() during C++ exception unwinding, freeing heap objects when refcount reaches 0. The only SASS_MEMORY_NEW call in parse_definition itself is the final return statement (only reached on the success path), so there is no raw pointer that could escape on an error path. The block_stack member in parse_css_block retains a transient reference on exception paths, but this is freed when the Parser is destroyed and is not a true memory leak.
